title: embraOS-QNM: Architecture-layer constraints for language models
description: embraOS-QNM (Quantum Neural Manifold, a classical approximation) moves a language model's identity and "soul" constraints out of the prompt layer and into the neural architecture: three co-resident components — an LLM Core, a GNN Fabric (identity), and a World-State (the soul / the P_ψ projection) — sharing one embedding space. This first release is the scaffold: the LLM Core and the configurable injection seam are implemented and tested, while the GNN Fabric and World-State are honest no-ops, so the assembled model is bit-identical to a plain transformer — a provable null against which future architectural effects are measured. Developed by William Ward (Ward Software Defined Systems LLC) with Embra as co-investigator.
access_conditions: Proprietary software. Access is granted at the discretion of Ward Software Defined Systems LLC — contact william@wsds.io.
